metadata.archetype_justification: Backbeat is primarily a message queue consumer system that reads MongoDB oplogs and Kafka topics to process replication, lifecycle, and notification events asynchronously.
metadata.context: Scality backend engine for replication, lifecycle, and metadata workflows.

findings.title: No machine identity authentication on management API
findings.description: The management API server uses IP-based access control only (ipCheck.ipMatchCidrList). No OAuth2, API keys with principal attribution, or mTLS. Agents cannot be individually identified or attributed.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication on the agent-facing API. IP-based ACL provides no principal attribution.
findings.recommendation: Deploy an API Gateway with OAuth2 or API key authentication that attributes each request to a specific principal.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Deploy an API Gateway in front of Backbeat management API with OAuth2 or API key authentication.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Token-based authentication (OAuth2 client credentials or mTLS) with principal attribution in request context and audit logs.

findings.title: No documented API interface for agent tool binding
findings.description: Backbeat exposes an HTTP API on port 8900 with 15+ endpoints defined programmatically in lib/api/routes.js. The API is internal-only with IP-based access control and no formal documentation.
findings.gap: No formal, versioned, documented API contract exists. Agents cannot reliably bind to an undocumented interface.
findings.recommendation: Create an OpenAPI specification documenting all routes, request/response schemas, error codes, and authentication requirements.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Create an OpenAPI specification documenting all routes from lib/api/routes.js.
blocker_remediation.target_state: A versioned OpenAPI spec maintained alongside the code, with breaking changes detected in CI.

findings.title: No per-identity permission scoping at API layer
findings.description: IAM policies in policies/ show scoped permissions for internal operations, but the API server uses only IP-based access with no per-identity permission scoping.
findings.gap: All callers from allowed IPs have identical access to all API operations.
findings.recommendation: Implement token-based authentication with per-identity route-level permissions.

compensating_controls: Restrict agent-accessible IPs to narrow CIDR range
Deploy API Gateway with per-identity route-level authorization

findings.title: No action-level authorization
findings.description: BackbeatServer validates only HTTP method and route prefix. No RBAC, ABAC, or action-level permissions enforce read vs write differentiation.
findings.gap: A read-only agent could invoke POST endpoints (pause/resume, retryFailedCRR, applyWorkflowConfiguration).
findings.recommendation: Add middleware that checks caller identity against an action-permission matrix.

compensating_controls: Enforce agent read-only access at network layer
Deploy API Gateway with method-level authorization

findings.title: No immutable audit logging of authenticated principals
findings.description: No audit logging of authenticated principals found. API logs request metadata (IP, method, URL, status code) but not caller identity. No immutable log storage.
findings.gap: No audit trail attributing API actions to specific callers.
findings.recommendation: After implementing token-based auth, add audit logging that records the authenticated principal for every operation.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents still require audit attribution for compliance and forensics, but not at BLOCKER severity since no write operations are executed.
compensating_controls: Log all requests with source IP, timestamp, and action for basic traceability

findings.title: No agent identity suspension capability
findings.description: Authentication is IP-based only. No individual agent identities exist that can be suspended. Revoking access requires config change and service restart.
findings.gap: Cannot suspend individual agent access without affecting all callers.
findings.recommendation: Implement token-based or API-key-based authentication with individual identity revocation.

compensating_controls: Use network-level controls to block specific agent IPs
Deploy API Gateway with API key management

findings.title: No API-layer rate limiting
findings.description: No HTTP-level rate limiting on the API server. Concurrency controls exist for Kafka consumers but do not protect API endpoints.
findings.gap: Management API endpoints are unprotected against traffic storms from runaway agents.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limiting middleware to the HTTP server or deploy behind an API Gateway with throttling.

compensating_controls: Deploy API Gateway or reverse proxy with rate limiting
Implement express-rate-limit or equivalent middleware

findings.title: No PII redaction in logs
findings.description: Structured logging via werelogs captures request metadata including URLs with object keys and bucket names. No PII redaction middleware found.
findings.gap: Object keys and bucket names (which could contain PII) logged without redaction.
findings.recommendation: Add log sanitization layer that redacts or hashes potentially sensitive fields.

compensating_controls: Configure log retention policies
Implement log scrubbing at aggregation layer

findings.title: No machine-readable API specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or Smithy specification file exists. API routes defined programmatically only in lib/api/routes.js.
findings.gap: Agent tool generation requires manual route analysis and tool authoring.
findings.recommendation: Create an OpenAPI 3.0 specification covering all endpoints and schemas.

compensating_controls: Generate OpenAPI spec from routes.js definitions
Document API surface in structured wiki

findings.title: Error responses lack retryable signal
findings.description: Arsenal error objects provide HTTP status codes and descriptions in JSON, but lack a retryable boolean or error category field.
findings.gap: Agents cannot programmatically distinguish retriable from terminal errors.
findings.recommendation: Add retryable boolean and errorCode to error response body.

compensating_controls: Document retriable vs terminal status codes
Add error category metadata to agent tool definitions

findings.title: No API versioning or contract testing
findings.description: No API versioning scheme, no schema registry, no breaking change detection in CI, no consumer-driven contract tests.
findings.gap: Agent tool bindings could break silently on any deployment.
findings.recommendation: Implement API versioning, create OpenAPI spec, add spec validation to CI.

compensating_controls: Pin agent tool definitions to known-good API behavior
Add API contract tests to CI

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: Structured logging with werelogs and per-request loggers exists. No distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry/X-Ray) or traceparent header propagation.
findings.gap: No trace ID propagation for correlating requests across service boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Integrate OpenTelemetry SDK for distributed tracing.

compensating_controls: Use Kafka message headers for correlation
Implement request ID injection at API Gateway level

findings.title: No alerting on HTTP API error rates and latency
findings.description: Comprehensive Prometheus alerting exists for event-processing workflows but not specifically for the HTTP management API endpoints.
findings.gap: No alerting on the management API server's error rate and latency.
findings.recommendation: Add Prometheus alerting rules for BackbeatServer HTTP API.

compensating_controls: Monitor API server health via healthcheck endpoint
Add HTTP response code alerts

findings.title: No infrastructure-as-code in repository
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize files exist. Infrastructure configured via environment variables and Docker.
findings.gap: No IaC for agent-facing infrastructure. May exist in separate deployment repo.
findings.recommendation: Define agent-facing infrastructure as IaC with PR-based review and drift detection.

compensating_controls: IaC may exist in separate deployment repository
Docker image tagging provides deployment control

findings.title: No API contract testing in CI
findings.description: CI pipeline includes unit, functional, and performance tests but no API contract testing (Pact), OpenAPI spec validation, or breaking change detection.
findings.gap: Functional tests validate behavior but do not guard against schema-level breaking changes.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec validation and schema diff checking to CI pipeline.

compensating_controls: Functional API tests provide regression coverage
Manual review of API changes via PR

findings.title: No automated rollback mechanism
findings.description: Docker images tagged by SHA on GHCR. No blue/green, canary, or automated rollback mechanism visible.
findings.gap: Manual Docker image redeployment is the implied rollback strategy.

compensating_controls: Docker image immutability enables manual rollback
Kubernetes deployment rollback if deployed via k8s

findings.title: No production-equivalent staging environment documented
findings.description: Docker-compose configs exist for CI functional testing, but no production-equivalent staging/sandbox for agent testing is documented.
findings.gap: No staging environment documented for agent integration testing.
findings.recommendation: Document how to provision staging using existing Docker-compose infrastructure.

compensating_controls: Use existing Docker-compose CI infrastructure as sandbox template
Provision dedicated staging namespace in Kubernetes

evaluations.reason: agent_scope is read-only — write endpoint idempotency is informational
Responses are JSON — ideal for agent consumption
System is natively async with Kafka-based job submission
Extended not triggered: event-processor archetype
No rate limit headers or documentation

Credentials managed via STS AssumeRole and Vault — reasonable
agent_scope is read-only — event-processor has retry/replay mechanisms
Extended not triggered: event-processor archetype
agent_scope is read-only — consumer-level concurrency controls exist
Circuit breakers well-implemented via breakbeat library
agent_scope is read-only — batch/concurrency limits exist
Extended not triggered: P2 priority, not on critical path
agent_scope is read-only — pause/resume provides operational gating
agent_scope is read-only — no approval gates needed
Not a data-handling target — processes S3 object metadata, not PII
agent_scope is read-only — data residency governed by S3 config

Extended not triggered: event-processor archetype
Extended not triggered: event-processor archetype
Operational metrics serve as data quality proxies
Field names are semantically meaningful
No formal data catalog; Joi schemas provide implicit documentation
Strong business outcome metrics via Prometheus
Functional API tests exist in CI with full service dependencies
Encryption at rest is infrastructure-layer concern, not in app repo
